title: VaultInfo
summary: Represents a vault in an encrypted cloud file system.
rawIndexableTextContent: Represents a vault in an encrypted cloud file system.  Overview A vault is a password-derived directory path that isolates encrypted data. Devices with different passwords will create different vaults, each invisible to the other. The vault path derivation is compatible with the ObjC Ensembles 2 CDEEncryptedCloudFileSystemVaultInfo.

summary: Returns a Boolean value indicating whether two values are not equal.
rawIndexableTextContent: Returns a Boolean value indicating whether two values are not equal.  A value to compare. Another value to compare. Discussion Inequality is the inverse of equality. For any values a and b, a != b implies that a == b is false. This is the default implementation of the not-equal-to operator (!=) for any type that conforms to Equatable.

title: EncryptedCloudFileSystem
summary: A cloud file system wrapper that encrypts files before uploading and decrypts them after downloading.
rawIndexableTextContent: A cloud file system wrapper that encrypts files before uploading and decrypts them after downloading.  Overview Files are stored in a password-derived vault directory with a .cdecrypt extension. This is compatible with the Ensembles 2 ObjC CDEEncryptedCloudFileSystem. By default, new data is encrypted with AES-256-GCM (modern format). Set encryptionFormat to .legacy for RNCryptor v3 compatibility. Decryption auto-detects the format.

title: init(password:salt:)
summary: Creates a vault info with the given password and optional salt.
rawIndexableTextContent: Creates a vault info with the given password and optional salt.  A non-empty password. An optional salt string. If nil or empty, the default salt is used. Return Value A VaultInfo, or nil if the password is empty.

title: EnsemblesEncrypted
summary: An encryption wrapper for any Ensembles cloud backend.
rawIndexableTextContent: An encryption wrapper for any Ensembles cloud backend. Overview EnsemblesEncrypted provides EncryptedCloudFileSystem, a wrapper that encrypts files before uploading and decrypts after downloading. It can wrap any CloudFileSystem implementation — CloudKit, Google Drive, WebDAV, or your own custom backend — to provide end-to-end encryption for sync data. Encrypted files are stored in a password-derived vault directory with a .cdecrypt extension. This is backward compatible with the Ensembles 2 Objective-C CDEEncryptedCloudFileSystem. Encryption Formats Two encryption formats are available: .modern (default) — AES-256-GCM. Fast, authenticated encryption using Apple’s CryptoKit. Recommended for new apps. .legacy — RNCryptor v3 format. Use this for backward compatibility with Ensembles 2 encrypted data. The backend auto-detects the encryption format when downloading, so a .modern client can read .legacy files and vice versa. The format setting only controls how new files are encrypted. Setup Wrap any cloud file system with encryption:  For more control over the vault, use the VaultInfo initializer:  How It Works Vault directory — All encrypted files are stored inside a vault directory in the cloud, derived from the password. Different passwords create different vaults, so the same cloud account can host multiple independent encrypted sync stores. Upload — When Ensembles uploads a file, the wrapper encrypts it to a local temporary file, uploads the encrypted version with a .cdecrypt extension, then removes the temporary file. Download — When Ensembles downloads a file, the wrapper downloads the encrypted .cdecrypt file, auto-detects the format (modern or legacy), decrypts it to a local temporary file, and returns the decrypted content. Backward compatibility — The .legacy format uses RNCryptor v3, the same encryption format used by Ensembles 2’s CDEEncryptedCloudFileSystem. Apps migrating from E2 to E3 can read existing encrypted cloud data without re-encrypting. Wrapping Other Backends The wrapper works with any CloudFileSystem:  Dependencies This module has no external dependencies beyond Foundation, CryptoKit, and the core Ensembles framework.
